Bright blue lobster caught off Portland coast   A rare, blazing blue lobster was hauled in by a fisherman off the coast of Maine over the weekend.
Toronto cops look to ID suspects in separate TTC robbery, sex assault   Both attacks follow dozens of random violent incidents on the TTC.
Madonna claims arrest threats over saucy tour antics   Pop star insisted her outrageous antics paved way for female artists today

With heavy hearts, Blue Jays fall to Tampa Bay Rays in series finale   Baseball came a distant second for the Blue Jays on Sunday.
Canadian star forward Cyle Larin leaves Turkey's Besiktas for Belgium's Club Brugge   Forward Cyle Larin is joining fellow Canadian Tajon Buchanan at Belgium’s Club Brugge.
Argos need to make a stand against Big Blue following last week's shellacking   The Argos looked in the mirror following last week’s drubbing in B.C., and were aghast at what they saw.

Harris puts time with Bombers in rear-view mirror as he prepares to face old team   TORONTO — Andrew Harris has worked on trying to make it seem like Monday’s match-up against the Winnipeg Blue Bombers is just another game.
GAMEDAY: Winnipeg Blue Bombers at Toronto Argonauts   Winnipeg Blue Bombers (3-0) at Toronto Argonauts (1-1)

Blue Jays vs A's Picks and Predictions: Manoah Provides Course Correction   The Toronto Blue Jays did something odd this weekend. They took the first two games of their series against the Tampa Bay Rays but lost that same series in a rare five-game set. Toronto will try to bounce back when it begins a seven-game West Coast road trip starting tonight against the Oakland Athletics.
Today’s MLB Prop Picks: Rodon Lights Up the Strikezone   Happy Fourth of July! May your day be full of barbeque, refreshing beverages, fireworks, and of course, winning MLB player prop bets.

Austrian Grand Prix Odds: Max Going for Record 4th Victory at Spielberg   The British Grand Prix was a thrilling race that saw Carlos Sainz finally claim his first Formula 1 victory. The Ferrari driver is in the midst of his most impressive season, reaching five podiums in nine races prior to Sunday’s win. Before 2022, Sainz had finished as high as second only twice since his debut in 2015.

British Grand Prix Picks and Predictions: Redemption for Verstappen at Silverstone   One of the crown jewels of the Formula 1 calendar will take place this weekend, as the sport descends on historic Silverstone for the British Grand Prix.

Today's Best Sportsbook Odds Boosts and Promotions: July 1   Finding the best sports betting value has become just a little bit easier, as most sportsbooks routinely offer daily odds boosts and promotions on select markets.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

CRIME HUNTER: Times Square Killer linked to 1968 murder   During the day, Richard Cottingham would toil as a computer programmer in Manhattan, across the Hudson River from his suburban New Jersey home.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

Ex-wife of slain Microsoft exec and father of four allegedly wanted to ‘shut him up’   The death of a Microsoft executive remains unsolved but a surprising new development came out of a Florida tattoo parlour.
HAVE YOUR SAY: Answer our daily Toronto Sun poll question

TERRAZZANO: Each Canadian owes $56,000 in government debt   Fifty-six thousand dollars. That’s the average amount each Canadian will owe in provincial and federal government debt by the end of the year.

AGAR: Protesters need to set goals to make their point   The problem with the current crop of protesters in Canada isn’t that they don’t have a point; that they don’t have a complaint. It is that they have too many.
EDITORIAL: A friendly toast to good neighbours   It’s a happy coincidence that within one seven-day period, Canadians and Americans mark and honour both countries’ national days.

ELDER: Liberal tolerance on display after Roe v. Wade reversal   Last week, the Supreme Court reversed Roe v. Wade, the 1973 decision that outlawed state anti-abortion laws and federalized an issue that, for all but the last 50 years of our history, was decided by the states.

Tom Cruise seen dangling off plane in wild stunt to celebrate 60th birthday   As Tom Cruise celebrated his milestone 60th birthday on Sunday, his Mission: Impossible director Christopher McQuarrie marked the occasion by sharing a photo of the action star dangling off the side of a plane in a stunt that will appear in the upcoming two-part conclusion to the long-running franchise.
Drake joins Backstreet Boys in Toronto for 'I Want It That Way'   Drake is now an honorary member of the Backstreet Boys.

Oscar-winner Paul Haggis released from house arrest in Italy, lawyer says   MILAN — An Italian judge on Monday ruled that Oscar-winning screenwriter and director Paul Haggis should be released from house arrest while authorities continue to investigate allegations that he raped a woman, his lawyer told Reuters.

Ricky Martin denies restraining order allegations   SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico — Puerto Rican superstar Ricky Martin on Sunday denied allegations that led to a restraining order against him, with police noting that he has not been charged with any crime.

Harry Styles devastated over Denmark shooting, cancels concert   LONDON — British singer Harry Styles said he was “heartbroken” and “devastated” over a deadly shooting at a shopping mall in Copenhagen on Sunday, after he cancelled a concert scheduled to take place in the city.

MOTHER OF PEARLE: Luxe getaway on the shores of Lake Ontario in Burlingon   While the easing of travel restrictions has opened up a world of possibilities, not everyone is ready to jet off to Fiji, Paris or Pittsburgh.

ASK AMY: Social media brings a honey into the house   Dear Amy: I was married for 46 years to a man who probably shouldn’t have been married at all. He was not affectionate, even during sex, cheated on me at least once, and when I asked why he married me, he said, “Because it was the thing to do.”
B.C. has most breweries in Western Canada but fewest beer drinkers. Why?   Buying ale now costs 6.8 per cent more than it did in the 2020 fiscal year, liquor prices rose much less, by 2.4 per cent.
SEX FILES: Recognizing unhealthy relationships and doing something about it   Why do we stay in relationships that don’t serve our best interests?
Hop on board to see Stratford, Ont. museum with Bieber exhibit   Visitors will be able to book a ride to the museum through Stratford Transit online or by phone.

SUNshine Girl Lesonjia   SUNshine Girl Lesonjia is helping us hit another crescendo. The Virgo hip-hop artist likes writing music, wants to be an entertainer and is a hoops fan, especially with all things Raptors. (Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Cephra   SUNshine Girl Cephra is a Libra who enjoys exploring cities, writing, studying and working out. When it's time to have fun, she says she likes to attend concerts. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)

SUNshine Girl Meg   SUNshine Girl Meg loves to vacation at the beach — any beach. The 5-foot Taurus, who first posed for us in October 2018, also loves ballet and dancing to hip-hop. (Ernest Doroszuk/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Marijana   SUNshine Girl Marijana is back to spice it up! The Red Hot Chili Peppers fan enjoys travelling, shopping, swimming and watching movies, while her dream car is a white BMW. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)

SUNshine Girl Chantelle   Here’s another shot of SUNshine Girl Chantelle. Our 5-foot-5, blue-eyed Sagittarius, who is back from her 2019 shoot, is into bartending and dreams of being a model. (Toronto Sun)
Balancing Act: Ontario driver flips Dodge Charger on Hwy 401 ramp   When asked to retrieve his backpack from the passenger’s seat, police found it filled with cannabis products
How recycled tires become new playgrounds   Tire Stewardship BC is funding 13 community projects this year

Multiple charges laid after firefighter dragged by driver of stolen vehicle   A Calgary Fire Department member assisting at the scene was dragged by the vehicle as the driver attempted to flee, according to police

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Machado: COVID has made socializing a little like having sex without protection   Getting back to some kind of normal means accepting the risk of COVID, and understanding that the feeling of liberation comes along with fear and uncertainty.
What are the signs of Parkinson's disease?   For people living with Parkinson's disease, exercise and eating a balanced diet may help control symptoms and even slow progression of the disease.
Clint Eastwood handed $2.6 million after winning a second trademark infringement CBD lawsuit   Company did not have permission to use his image or likeness.
Cannabis grow worth half a million dollars discovered in century-old church   About 350 cannabis plants found in church built in 1875.

Canadian cannabis giant Tilray offers U.S. workers coverage for abortion-related costs   Updated health care benefits will cover transportation expenses for employees and their covered family members.
WNBA star Brittney Griner goes on trial in Russia on drug charges   Griner, 31, could serve up to 10 years in a Russian jail if convicted of drug charges.
Ontario man accused of impaired driving after cannabis and alcohol found inside his vehicle   Man taken into custody after the OPP received multiple complaints about his driving on Highway 12
